title: Do not use success-green for trivial operations
summary: AI-generated interfaces use green success indicators (toasts, badges, border highlights) for every completed operation — including trivial saves, filter applications, and selections — diluting the signal value of the color.
instruction: Reserve green success indicators for operations where completion is non-obvious, confirmation is legally required, or the operation's outcome would otherwise be uncertain. For operations with visible inline results (text saved, item selected), use no confirmation or a subtle non-color indicator.
rationale: When green appears after every operation, it loses meaning as a success signal. Users learn to ignore it, and genuinely important confirmations (payment sent, account deleted) compete with trivial confirmations for attention. Green as a universal 'done' signal inflates the perceived achievement of routine operations.
violations: Green success toast after every form save operation
Green border highlight on every successfully validated field
Green checkmark on every completed step regardless of step significance
Green status badge for operations that have no alternative outcome
Success color applied reflexively when the operation result is visible inline
controls: No confirmation for operations with visible inline results
Subtle gray or neutral indicator instead of green for routine completions
Green reserved for operations where completion is uncertain or significant
Tone-muted success communication ('Saved' in neutral gray instead of green badge)
borderlineCases: Multi-step processes where each step completion is genuinely useful feedback
contextualExceptions: Legal or financial transactions where explicit completion confirmation is required
detectorSignal: Green-colored UI elements (checkmarks, badges, toasts, highlights) appearing after user-initiated operations that have visible inline outcomes.
matcherProposal: Detect green-colored notification elements (toasts, badges, icons) that appear after form submissions or saves, particularly when the result is already visible in the interface.
detectorLimitations: Cannot determine the significance of the operation from the interface alone
falsePositiveScenarios: Operations where explicit green confirmation is genuinely helpful
mitigationStrategy: Focus detection on green indicators for operations where the outcome is visually obvious inline.

fixturePlans.violation: Green 'Profile saved!' toast appearing after every settings save, even though the saved values are now visible in the form.
fixturePlans.control: Save button text changes to 'Saved' in neutral gray for 2 seconds, no toast, no green color.
fixturePlans.borderline: Payment confirmation with green checkmark and transaction ID where explicit confirmation is expected.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Related to notifications.toast-fatigue (P-CC-021) but focuses on the color signal specifically rather than the notification format.
